name: Lumpy Skin Disease
description: A viral disease of cattle characterised by nodular skin lesions, fever, and generalised lymphadenopathy. Spread primarily by biting insects.
clinical_signs: Firm, raised skin nodules (2–5 cm diameter)
Oedema of the limbs, brisket, and genitalia
recommendations: Contact veterinarian urgently – LSD is a notifiable disease
Do NOT administer medications without veterinary guidance
Quarantine the herd from new arrivals
Report to local animal health authorities
Document all lesion locations with photographs
Ensure adequate nutrition and fresh water
references: OIE Terrestrial Animal Health Code – Chapter 11.8
FAO Animal Production and Health Guidelines – LSD
CFSPH Lumpy Skin Disease Fact Sheet 2019
